completeness: AC IDs missing: none — 100% coverage. Negative ACs covered per user story (AC-1.4, AC-2.2, AC-2.4, AC-3.2 through AC-3.5, AC-4.3, AC-5.2, AC-5.3, AC-6.3). No Scenario Outline used anywhere; boundary cases beyond AC list (e.g. fine at 19 vs 20 days late) absent.
alignment: @AC-1.1 Scenario: The catalog is sorted case-insensitively (us-1-catalog.feature:12-13)
@AC-3.2 Scenario: Members cannot exceed five active loans — name is human-readable restatement of AC (us-3-lending.feature:22-23)
Bidirectional traceability: all 29 AC IDs map to exactly one scenario; all 29 scenarios carry unique @AC-x.y tags. Background sections remove setup duplication in all 6 feature files.
declarative: Good: "When the librarian lends 'Dune' to Alice" , "Then Alice has 1 active loan", "Given Alice reserves 'Alpha'" , "Then Bob's reservation for 'Dune' is 'notified'" (domain language: lend, return, reserve, fulfil).

Bad: "Then the response status is 404", "Then the response status is 201", "Then the response status is 409", "Then the response status is 403", "Then the response status is 402", "Then the response status is 200" — HTTP status codes appear in nearly every scenario, violating declarative/business-level voice.
atomic: Given Alice has borrowed 'Dune'" — one action per step. "And Alice's loan for 'Dune' is 3 days overdue" — separate step, properly chained. No compound "and ... and ..." within a single Given/When/Then found.
